text.title: QA Test Strategy Template Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the qa-test-strategy-template methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Strategy opens with a numbered risk list (≥3 risks). Tests are listed under the risks they mitigate, not standalone.
rule.rationale: Test-first strategies enumerate tools, not coverage; risk-first strategies prove the tests address something.

rule.statement: Each risk is mapped to one or more layers ∈ {unit, integration, contract, e2e, perf, security, accessibility}.

rationale.source: Mike Cohn pyramid; layered testing canon.

rule.rationale: Without layers it's unclear who owns the work and where the test lives.

rule.statement: Each layer in scope has a binary exit criterion (e.g. unit coverage > 80% on changed files; e2e smoke pack green).
rule.rationale: Soft exit criteria let releases ship before tests are honestly done.

rule.statement: Out-of-scope items are named explicitly with a reason. 'Not testing' is itself a decision and must be recorded.
rule.rationale: Implicit out-of-scope creates dispute when incidents happen.

rule.statement: Each layer has a single named owner accountable for sign-off.
Strategy fits on one page (≤1000 words). Longer strategies are split into appendices but the page-1 stays canonical.
rule.rationale: Multi-page strategies don't get read; one-page strategies get read and acted on.

rule.statement: If change is trivial bug fix OR org template covers same shape OR spike code with no QA contract, skip this methodology.
rule.rationale: Strategy ceremony is wasted in those configurations.
